I do concur with option C - reverse total shoulder replacement being the best course of action here. For younger patients, while acromioplasty might address some symptoms temporarily, its long-term efficacy is questionable due to continued wear-and-tear on remaining tissues. Additionally, given the severity implied by "irreparable" tears, the comprehensive benefits offered by reverse shoulder replacements—enhanced stability and functional improvement—are more likely to provide sustained relief and quality-of-life enhancement than tendon transfers alone could achieve without risking further complications from poor healing conditions. Thus, maintaining robust upper arm muscle support through prosthetic components aligns well with restoring optimal shoulder biomechanics needed post-surgery.
